data.title: Skyworks appoints Robert Schriesheim as interim CFO
data.description:
• Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) said on Thursday that Mark Dentinger will not be joining Skyworks as Chief Financial Officer due to an unforeseen medical condition. 
  • Robert Schriesheim, a member of Skyworks’ board, has been appointed interim CFO while Skyworks conducts a comprehensive search for a successor.
  • Schriesheim has been a director of Skyworks since 2006 and currently serves as chairman of Truax Partners partnering with CEOs, boards and institutional investors as a private investor and director. 
  • Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) shares were up 1.01% in extended trading.

data.title: PagerDuty Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.05, revenue of $119.81M beats by $0.83M
data.description:
• PagerDuty press release (NYSE:PD): FQ1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $119.81M (+7.8% Y/Y) beats by $0.83M.
  • Shares +0.03%.
  • Loss from operations was $10.3 million; operating margin was negative 8.6%.
  • Non-GAAP operating income was $24.4 million; non-GAAP operating margin was 20.3%.
  • Net loss per share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders was $0.07.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $30.7 million; free cash flow was $29.0 million.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and investments were $597.1 million as of April 30, 2025.

  • Financial Outlook

    For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, PagerDuty currently expects:

    • Total revenue of $122.5 million - $124.5 million, representing a growth rate of 6% - 7% year over year.
    • Non-GAAP net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders of $0.19 - $0.20 assuming approximately 94 million diluted shares and a non-GAAP tax rate of 22%.

    For the full fiscal year 2026, PagerDuty currently expects:

    • Total revenue of $493.0 million - $499.0 million (compared to the previous guidance of $500.0 million - $507.0 million), representing a growth rate of 5% - 7% year over year.
    • Non-GAAP net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders of $0.95 - $1.00 (up from $0.90 - $0.95) assuming approximately 93 million diluted shares and a non-GAAP tax rate of 22%.
    • FQ2 EPS consensus of $0.23
    • FQ2 revenue consensus of $123.81M
    • FY EPS consensus of $0.93
    • FY revenue consensus of $503.26M

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: NVDA, DJT, VKTX, BRK.A, BEAM
data.description:
Here are the latest stock rating upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Sell to buy by The Techie. "China remains a significant uncertainty, but robust demand for Blackwell products and global AI infrastructure ambitions support my bullish case. Despite valuation being less attractive than in April, Nvidia's leadership in AI and strong product roadmap make it a buy in my book." 

Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B). Hold to buy by Envision Research.

Trump Media (NASDAQ:DJT). Sell to hold by Mike Fay. "I'm upgrading DJT from 'sell' to 'hold' due to its recent $2.5 billion Bitcoin treasury announcement. Despite my prior bearish stance, DJT's valuation concerns remain, but the Bitcoin move introduces a new angle that could limit the stock's downside."

Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX). Sell to hold by Stephen Ayers. "Viking Therapeutics faces tough competition in the obesity drug market dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), with first-mover advantages hard to overcome…Despite some appeal for risk-tolerant investors, the odds are against Viking, so I rate the stock as a Hold, given the challenging landscape."

Beam Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BEAM). Sell to hold by Stephen Ayers.

Criteo (NASDAQ:CRTO). Hold to buy by Sandeep Nital David.

PBF Energy (PRD). Sell to hold by Ronald Ferrie.

data.title: PCE inflation seen ticking up in April, as tariff effects filter into data
data.description:
Friday brings another glimpse into how much of an issue inflation is in the U.S. economy. The PCE price index is expected to tick up 0.1% M/M in April vs. flat in March, according to the consensus of economists. The core PCE price index, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is also expected to edge up 0.1% in April vs. 0.0% in the prior month.

On a year-over-year basis, headline PCE prices are anticipated to rise 2.1%, slowing from the 2.3% rate in March, while core PCE is expected to hold at 2.6%.

Evercore ISI's Stan Shipley estimates that core PCE deflator increased 0.16% M/M in April. "There are some signs that 'hard' economic data is starting to feel the influence of tariffs and economic uncertainty," he wrote in a note to clients.

"The Y/Y gains for the headline and core PCE deflators likely slipped lower in April. However, those Y/Y gains will start to climb higher when the May/June gains are reported," Shipley added.

The Federal Reserve prefers to use core PCE to gauge the underlying trend in inflation.

Friday's report will also offer more data on consumers' purchasing power. Personal consumption expenditures, or consumer spending, is expected to rise 0.2% M/M, slowing from the 0.7% increase in March. Personal income growth is also expected to slow — to 0.3% M/M from 0.5% in March.

The Cleveland Fed inflation nowcast projects April PCE rose 0.19% M/M and 2.23% Y/Y, with core PCE rising 0.21% M/M and 2.60% Y/Y.

Still, the month's report will only start to show the effects of President Trump's tariffs. The full effect of any tariffs aren't expected until later in the summer, as business inventories that were purchased in February and March will have rolled off by then.

data.title: Baker Hughes, C3 AI to expand joint venture through 2028
data.description:
Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) +1.6% in Thursday's trading after saying its joint venture agreement with C3 AI (AI) to develop, deliver, and market Enterprise AI solutions to the oil and gas and chemical industries will be renewed and expanded through June 2028.

Since establishing their JV in 2019, Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) and C3 AI (AI) said they have deployed Enterprise AI solutions to Shell, Eni, QatarEnergy LNG, Petronas, ExxonMobil, LyondellBasell, Flint Hills Resources, and others, delivering efficiency and sustainability gains by optimizing production, reducing unplanned downtime, and enhancing operational visibility.

Under the renewal, the companies will increase market activities, continuing and expanding work across the energy and industrial sectors.

"It's difficult to overestimate the importance of the Baker Hughes partnership to C3 AI and its shareholders," C3 AI (AI) Chairman and CEO Thomas Siebel said, noting the JV has generated more than $500M in revenues from the O&G and chemical markets.

Additionally, Baker Hughes (BKR) said it won an award to supply 16 NovaLT gas turbines to Frontier Infrastructure Holdings to power the company's data center projects in Wyoming and Texas, delivering up to 270 MW of power.

data.title: Canopy Growth Q4 Preview: What to expect?
data.description:
Ontario-based cannabis company Canopy Growth Corporation (NASDAQ:CGC) is set to report fourth quarter earnings results on Friday. May 30th, after the closing bell.

Analysts on consensus expect the company to report revenue of C$70.87M, with a non-GAAP loss per share of C$0.20. 

Canopy Growth (CGC) announced last month that the company has made an optional early prepayment under its senior secured term loan in an aggregate principal amount equal to $100M at a discounted price of $97.5M. This would result in interest expense savings of about $13M on an annualized basis. 

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system and sell-side analysts have rated the stock as a HOLD, while SA authors recommend SELL. 

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen one downward revision, while revenue estimates have seen two downward revisions. 

Seeking Alpha analyst Alan Brochstein argued in a recent analysis that Canopy Growth’s business model is broken, with persistent losses, weak leadership, and failed M&A, making its long-term outlook bleak. 

“Despite a recent rally, CGC's valuation remains unattractive compared to peers, trading at a high multiple and only slightly below tangible book value,” the analyst said. 

Canopy Growth (CGC) shares have declined more than 36% so far this year, compared to a 0.12% gain in the broader benchmark index.

data.title: UK government prepares to sell final stake in NatWest Group - report
data.description:
The UK government is preparing to sell its remaining stake in NatWest Group (NYSE:NWG), which amounts to roughly 0.1%, Sky News reported, citing sources.

A public statement can be released either later on Friday or on Monday morning, the May 29 report noted.

A sale is likely on Friday, but the timings are still subject to change, the sources reportedly said.

The disposal of the Treasury's final interest in the lender is said to have been anticipated for weeks.

NatWest, formerly known as Royal Bank of Scotland, was bailed out with £45.5B of taxpayers' money in 2008, Sky News noted.

British taxpayers face a loss of over £10B on the 2008 rescue of the lender, with proceeds from share sales, dividends, and fees expected to total about £35.3B, Sky News had reported last week.

data.title: BRP anticipates double-digit top-line growth potential in H2 fiscal 2026 as inventory rightsizing nears completion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BRP Inc. (DOOO) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jose Boisjoli, President and CEO, stated the company delivered a sound performance in the first quarter, with results in line with expectations as BRP continued to rightsize network inventory and "execute on elements within our control." Boisjoli noted, "The operating environment remained challenging with significant macroeconomic uncertainty and a volatile tariff situation affecting consumer confidence. Still driven by very solid snowmobile sales, we slightly outperformed the Powersport North American industry at retail."
  • The CEO highlighted progress in the Marine Group, mentioning the sale of Alumacraft and a definitive agreement for the sale of Telwater, while the process for Manitou is ongoing. He emphasized, "We ended the first quarter with revenue of $1.8 billion, normalized EBITDA of $201 million, normalized EPS of $0.47 and strong free cash flow generation of $162 million."
  • Boisjoli announced his intention to retire by the end of the fiscal year after 22 years as CEO, stating, "The time has come for me to hand over the wheel to a new leader."
  • CFO Sebastien Martel said, "This quarter was another demonstration of our team's ability to execute with discipline in a dynamic environment, putting us in a good position early in fiscal '26, with results in line with our expectations, solid free cash flow generation and continued improvement on the network and the inventory front."

Outlook

  • Martel indicated that Q2 is expected to be "our last quarter of significant network inventory reduction," with financial performance for the quarter projected to be similar to Q1.
  • Regarding the second half of the year, Martel said, "In a scenario where the retail environment remains consistent with what we have seen in Q1, these elements could yield double-digit top-line growth along with strong improvement and normalized EBITDA and EPS compared to the second half of last year."
  • Management refrained from issuing full-year guidance, citing ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and evolving tariff environments as limiting visibility.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the quarter was $1.8 billion, with normalized EBITDA of $201 million and normalized EPS of $0.47. Free cash flow generation totaled $162 million from continued operations, and the company ended the quarter with over $300 million in cash.
  • Gross profit was reported as $395 million, with a margin of 21.4%, "down from last year primarily due to the less efficient use of our assets, given the lower production volumes, higher sales programs, unfavorable model mix and foreign exchange headwinds," according to Martel.
  • Network inventory was reduced by 21% compared to the previous year, with double-digit declines across all product lines and dealer credit line usage just above 70%, which Martel described as "the lowest level in over two years and below pre-COVID utilization rates."
  • The total gross tariff impact for fiscal '26 is currently estimated at $60 million to $70 million, which management expects to be manageable through cost mitigation strategies.

Q&A

  • Sabahat Khan, RBC Capital, asked for details on inventory rightsizing and remaining reductions. Martel responded that "inventories were down 21% and we've seen double-digit inventory declines across all of the product categories," adding that most heavy lifting on inventory reduction should be complete by the end of Q2.
  • Khan followed up on H2 retail outlook. Boisjoli explained confidence in the setup for H2, citing, "We are in better shape than others and we're confident for H2."
  • James Hardiman, Citi, inquired about retail trends and tariff impacts. Boisjoli said, "There is no big change in the trend versus the last few quarters" and noted premium vehicles are selling better than entry-level products, with tariffs expected to be manageable through mitigation efforts.
  • Benoit Poirier, Desjardins, asked about EBITDA margin prospects for H2. Martel stated, "The EBITDA margin is going to improve, but we're far, we're going to be away from what we are targeting in terms of overall EBITDA margin in the long-term."
  • Craig Kennison, Baird, asked about the breakdown of tariff impacts. Martel clarified that "about half of the impact comes from our P&A business and probably, let's say, a half of that is the China impact tariffs."
  • Robin Farley, UBS, questioned retail assumptions for inventory alignment. Martel said, "With the ongoing threat of tariffs, the volatile environment, we are seeing consumers hesitant to purchase."
  • Multiple analysts asked about credit trends and new product launches. Martel said dealer credit approvals have not materially changed, but lower-tier financiers are more selective, impacting entry-level product demand.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was cautious, pressing for more detail on inventory, retail trends, and the impact of tariffs and promotions, often seeking clarity on guidance and margin potential.
  • Management's sentiment was measured and focused on execution, frequently emphasizing confidence in inventory management and product launches, but consistently described the operating environment as "challenging" and "uncertain."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained cautious with similar references to uncertainty, but there was increased emphasis on the expected benefits of new product launches and inventory normalization in H2.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured the announcement of CEO Boisjoli's pending retirement, a notable management change not present in the previous quarter.
  • Inventory reduction efforts accelerated, with a 21% year-over-year decrease in network inventory compared to 13% at the end of the previous quarter.
  • While both quarters highlighted macroeconomic and tariff headwinds, the current quarter provided a more concrete estimate of tariff impact ($60 million to $70 million), up from a $40 million estimate previously.
  • The strategic narrative shifted to highlight the expected completion of inventory rightsizing by end of Q2 and the potential for double-digit top-line growth in H2, whereas the previous quarter refrained from directional projections.
  • Analysts continued to focus on inventory, tariffs, and margins, but there was increased questioning on the specifics of new product launches and their anticipated impact in the second half.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified "significant macroeconomic uncertainty and a volatile tariff situation affecting consumer confidence."
  • The evolving tariff environment, particularly U.S.-China tariffs, was cited as a business risk, with a full-year gross impact of $60 million to $70 million expected.
  • Softer demand in EMEA and Asia Pacific and reliance on premium segment performance were highlighted as ongoing concerns.
  • Analysts raised questions about the promotional environment, competitive inventory dynamics, and the impact of changing credit conditions on entry-level products.
  • Management is addressing risks by working with suppliers to reduce tariff impacts, aligning production with retail demand, and limiting price increases on model year 2025 products.

Final Takeaway

BRP management emphasized the company's disciplined execution amid a challenging environment, with inventory rightsizing nearly complete and a strong product launch pipeline expected to drive momentum in the second half of fiscal 2026. While refraining from issuing full-year guidance due to ongoing uncertainty, the company signaled confidence in the potential for double-digit top-line growth and margin improvement in H2, supported by leaner inventory, healthy dealer networks, and market-leading product innovation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sector snapshot spotlights Industrials as the top 2025 S&P performer
data.description:
As markets rebound from April’s volatility, Industrials have emerged as the top-performing sector in the S&P 500 for 2025, outpacing peers amid a broad-based recovery.

With seven of the index’s 11 sectors in positive territory year-to-date, Consumer Discretionary has notably lagged behind. This shift highlights evolving investor sentiment and economic momentum heading into midyear. Seeking Alpha’s latest sector snapshot offers a timely look at where strength and weakness lie in the market. 

See below Wall Street’s sectors ranked by year-to-date performances. 

No. 11: Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLY), -4.3% YTD. No.

10: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLE), -4.2% YTD.

No. 9: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV), -4.1% YTD. 

No. 8: Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLK), -0.5% YTD.
